Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course prepares students for professional finance jobs, supporting decent work (Target 8.5) and strengthening domestic financial institutions (Target 8.10) to foster economic growth.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course covers market efficiency and ethics, which relates to Target 10.5 (Improve the regulation and monitoring of global financial markets and institutions).

Climate Action.reason: The stated core competence on 'sustainability issues' infers the inclusion of climate change as a key financial risk, relevant to integrating climate measures into planning (Target 13.2).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The focus on market efficiency and ethics supports the development of accountable and transparent financial institutions (Target 16.6) and helps reduce corruption (Target 16.5).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course enhances understanding of global financial systems, contributing to macroeconomic stability (Target 17.13) and policy coherence for sustainable development (Target 17.14) via its ethics component.
